key_risks: A hotter-than-expected CPI or PPI release could push yields higher and trigger a sharp valuation reset in AI and growth equities.
The June FOMC could sound more hawkish than expected despite markets pricing a hold, pressuring high-duration technology themes.
Recent extreme momentum in cybersecurity, semiconductors, South Korea, and metals/mining could reverse abruptly from profit-taking or crowded positioning.
Any negative semiconductor supply-chain, export-control, or geopolitical news involving Korea or Taiwan could disproportionately hurt the portfolio.
A broad risk-off move from weak labor or consumer data could cause high-beta thematic equities to underperform the S&P 500.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest combination of recent momentum and fundamental support, with Q1 global semiconductor sales up sharply and AI infrastructure capex expectations still accelerating.
Very strong 7-day and 30-day relative momentum, supported by rising AI-related cybersecurity spending forecasts and likely continued investor preference for AI-adjacent software themes.
Diversified AI exposure benefits from large forecast AI spending growth and broad participation in the same leadership theme driving recent market outperformance.
Exceptional recent relative strength and heavy semiconductor/export sensitivity make it a high-beta way to capture ongoing AI and chip-cycle momentum over the one-month window.
Recent strength is supported by high inflation, firm industrial data, and forecasts for base-metals prices to reach all-time highs, adding cyclical commodity-equity exposure.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio deliberately overweights high-momentum AI, semiconductor, and related cyclical beneficiaries to maximize one-month upside versus the S&P 500. Macro data show resilient growth and easy financial conditions despite sticky inflation, which favors trend-following risk assets if the June data and FOMC do not trigger a sharp rates shock.
rationale_summary: Concentrated exposure to semiconductors, cybersecurity, broad AI, Korea, and metals/mining targets the strongest current price leadership with plausible near-term catalysts. The allocation accepts high volatility because the objective is one-month relative return rather than capital preservation.
